text: Reproduce: `su - ci -c 'echo ok'` prints 'This account is currently not available'. That message comes from `/usr/sbin/nologin` being set as the account's shell.
Confirm the shell: `getent passwd ci` — the last field is `/usr/sbin/nologin`. A service account that needs to run login-shell jobs needs a real shell.
Give it a valid shell: `sudo usermod -s /bin/bash ci` (or `/bin/sh`). Verify with `su - ci -c 'echo ok'`.
